Mortgage payment posting - paired with checking debit

Transfer to another account - verify it's not an expense
Billing reversal/correction that nets to zero - not a real expense or income

Personal payment to a family member - not an expense, internal
Family member's account - not operated by primary user, exclude all activity
Reversal of a fraudulent or disputed charge - not real income
Fraudulent/disputed charge (later reversed) - exclude both the charge and reversal

Cancelled airline booking refunded same/next day - use the full reference number from the transaction name so the specific rule (longer) beats the generic airline keyword rule (shorter). Both the charge and refund rows are excluded.

Charge reversed days later where both description variants share a common substring - single keyword excludes both if that substring is unique to this merchant. Confirm no other transactions from this merchant should remain visible.

When a provider has two transactions with identical description text but different amounts (e.g. $103.50 original charge + $10.00 real co-pay from same merchant), keyword rules cannot distinguish them. Use the categorize_billing_corrections() special-case function in filters.py instead — it checks name AND abs(amount) together. Add a tuple (KEYWORD, TARGET_AMOUNT) to the CORRECTIONS list in that function.
